subject: Public Health
title: Cohort Studies: Following People Forward
description: A quick win: understand Cohort Studies: Following People Forward.

hook.text: The Framingham Heart Study has followed one town's residents since 1948—revealing most of what we know about heart.

buildup.text: Cohort studies follow a group of people over time—comparing those exposed to a risk factor with those who aren't.

discovery.text: Because they track exposure before disease, cohort studies can establish temporal sequence—exposure came first.

twist.text: Cohort studies are expensive and take years—but they provide the strongest observational evidence for causation.

climax.text: The Nurses' Health Study—following 120,000 nurses—revealed links between hormone therapy, diet, and cancer.

punchline.text: Follow people long enough and disease patterns reveal themselves.

quiz.question: What is a key strength of cohort studies?
options: They establish that exposure came before disease
